secondary_archetype: People manager / org builder for enterprise TPM function

level_signals: 10+ years in Technical Program Management or Engineering Leadership
at least 4+ years in a people management capacity leading other TPMs or Engineers
Recruit, hire, and mentor a high-performing team of Technical Program Managers
Comp range $241,600–$302,000 base, consistent with Director-level at a high-growth AI company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in Technical Program Management or Engineering Leadership, with at least 4+ years in a people management capacity leading other TPMs or Engineers.
at least 4+ years in a people management capacity leading other TPMs or Engineers.
Proven track record of building teams from scratch in high-growth, fast-paced environments (e.g., Series C through IPO or within a major tech incumbent).
Deep expertise in the AI/ML lifecycle, including LLM fine-tuning, performance evaluation, and the infrastructure requirements for deploying GenAI at scale.
Expert-level Stakeholder Management: Ability to navigate complex organizational politics and drive consensus among diverse technical and non-technical leaders.
Systems Thinking: The ability to look beyond individual projects to see the broader technical ecosystem and identify "force multipliers" for the organization.
Strategic Tooling Proficiency: Experience implementing enterprise-level planning and tracking frameworks (e.g., Jira Align, specialized roadmap software, or custom internal dashboards).

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Engineering Background: A background as a Software or ML Engineer, providing the "technical gravity" needed to earn the respect of senior architects and principals.
Platform-First Mindset: Experience building and driving adoption of internal developer platforms or horizontal services used by thousands of internal stakeholders.
Change Management Expertise: Proven ability to lead large-scale organizational shifts in methodology or technical architecture.

scope.surface: Enterprise TPM function — building and scaling a world-class TPM team from the ground up across all business units
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise AI data and infrastructure platform
core_responsibilities: Recruit, hire, and mentor a high-performing team of Technical Program Managers
Define the long-term execution strategy for Enterprise programs, ensuring alignment with company-wide OKRs and product milestones
Establish standardized processes for program delivery, risk management, and cross-functional communication
Serve as the primary bridge between Engineering, Product, and Executive leadership
Translate complex technical roadmaps and GenAI initiatives into clear business outcomes for the C-suite and Board
Drive the adoption of enterprise-grade standards for data quality and infrastructure reliability

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Compensation range clearly disclosed ($241,600–$302,000 base) with equity mentioned
High-impact 'build from scratch' leadership opportunity with organizational design authority
Clear articulation of scope — not just managing programs but architecting a new organizational capability
Scale AI is a well-funded, high-profile AI infrastructure company with marquee clients
Role has true Director-level cross-functional authority including C-suite and Board exposure
red_flags: All requirements listed under 'Ideally You'd Have' rather than 'Required', creating ambiguity about which are truly hard requirements
No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
90-day waiting period policy for reconsidering same-role candidates noted explicitly
Location appears to be San Francisco onsite only; no remote or hybrid language stated
High buzzword density in JD may indicate organizational ambiguity or aspirational role definition
